source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
About PactFi

Private asset markets (PE, Private Credit, VC, Real Estate) have 10x to 9.8T in AUM over the past decade and are projected to grow to $17T in the next five years. However, digital infrastructure has not kept pace, with most of the market operating predominantly in error-prone, internal-only software solutions.



PactFi provides secure, end-to-end, operational infrastructure for managing complex private credit transactions. Our web-based application brings together all parties involved in such a transaction to more efficiently allocate capital, complete KYC, share documents, manage funds flow, and more. The platform is secured to a bank-grade standard, and we have received our SOC 2 Type 2 attestation.



PactFi was developed in close partnership with two of the industry's largest players, both of whom represent the top 3 players in the private credit space by both size (AUM) and deal activity.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Director of Engineering to lead high-performing teams and drive predictable, high-quality delivery as we scale beyond Series A. This role is critical in evolving our engineering organization from early-stage execution to a more structured, scalable system—without losing speed.

You’ll bring experience building and scaling engineering teams in a high-growth environment, while helping us become an AI-first engineering organization—leveraging modern AI tooling and workflows to accelerate development, improve quality, and rethink how software is built.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Lead Delivery Through Growth
- Own end-to-end delivery of critical product initiatives across multiple teams
- Drive predictable execution as the organization scales in size and complexity
- Introduce the right level of structure without slowing teams down
- Balance speed, scope, and quality in a fast-moving environment

Scale the Engineering Organization
- Build and evolve team structures as we grow from Series A into later stages
- Hire, develop, and lead Engineering Managers and engineers
- Establish clear ownership boundaries, accountability, and team autonomy
- Coach emerging leaders and help level up management across the org

Build Scalable Systems for Execution
- Evolve engineering processes from early-stage to scalable
- Introduce delivery metrics (predictability, cycle time, throughput)
- Ensure strong engineering fundamentals: testing, reliability, production readiness

Build an AI-First Engineering Culture
- Drive adoption of AI-assisted development tools
- Identify and implement opportunities to improve velocity and quality through AI workflows
- Establish best practices for safe, effective AI usage
- Evaluate and integrate tools based on measurable impact

Partner Cross-Functionally During Scale
- Work closely with Product and Design to translate priorities into plans
- Bring clarity and structure to roadmap planning
- Communicate tradeoffs, risks, and progress clearly

Collaborate on Technical & Platform Evolution
- Partner with senior technical leadership on architecture and platform
- Ensure teams adopt shared infrastructure effectively
- Balance near-term delivery with long-term scalability




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:




Stage-Specific Experience (Critical)
- Experience scaling an engineering org from Series A through growth
- Experience growing from few teams to multiple teams/org layers
- Ability to introduce structure without over-engineering
- Experience transitioning to distributed ownership

Experience
- 8+ years software engineering
- 3+ years leadership (including managing managers)
- Proven track record in high-growth environments

Leadership & Execution
- Strong operator with consistent delivery
- Experience building teams, not just inheriting
- Strong judgment on process vs speed
- Ability to manage increasing complexity

AI & Modern Engineering Mindset
- Experience integrating tools that measurably improve productivity
- Practical understanding of AI-assisted development tools in production

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET)
source_jobs.description:
About PactFi

Private asset markets (PE, Private Credit, VC, Real Estate) have 10x to 9.8T in AUM over the past decade and are projected to grow to $17T in the next five years. However, digital infrastructure has not kept pace, with most of the market operating predominantly in error-prone, internal-only software solutions.



PactFi provides secure, end-to-end, operational infrastructure for managing complex private credit transactions. Our web-based application brings together all parties involved in such a transaction to more efficiently allocate capital, complete KYC, share documents, manage funds flow, and more. The platform is secured to a bank-grade standard, and we have received our SOC 2 Type 2 attestation.



PactFi was developed in close partnership with two of the industry's largest players, both of whom represent the top 3 players in the private credit space by both size (AUM) and deal activity.

We are seeking a Senior Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) to help scale end-to-end test automation for a modern fintech platform.

This role is both hands-on and strategic, requiring expertise in:

 - Playwright-based automation engineering

 - Distributed system validation (API, UI, data layers)

 - AI-assisted testing and automation practices

Responsible for transforming test automation into an AI-augmented quality system to enable:

 - Faster, reliable releases with solid understanding of CI/CD and modern DevOps practices

 - Strong regression signal with minimal noise

 - Scalable and maintainable test coverage across critical business workflows

 

Automation Strategy & Leadership

 - Own design and evolution of automation testing framework (primarily Playwright)

 - Establish best practices for E2E, integration, and regression testing

 - Drive test coverage across critical financial workflows (deal lifecycle, cash flows, reporting, etc.)

 - Mentor engineers and QA contributors; set test quality and maintainability standards

 

Hands-On Engineering

 - Build and maintain robust Playwright test suites (TypeScript/JavaScript)

 - Develop reusable testing utilities, fixtures, and CI/CD integrations

 - Implement parallelization, test isolation, and flakiness mitigation strategies

 - Integrate tests into CI pipelines (e.g., GitHub Actions, CircleCI)

 

Product & Domain Alignment

 - Translate complex financial workflows (e.g., loan servicing, covenant tracking, capital calls) into deterministic test cases

 - Validate data integrity across systems (APIs, databases, reporting layers)

 - Collaborate with product and domain experts to identify edge cases and risk scenarios

 

Quality & Reliability

 - Define SLAs for test reliability and pipeline performance

 - Monitor and improve test signal vs. noise

 - Introduce observability into testing (logs, traces, artifacts)

 - Lead incident retrospectives related to quality gaps

 

AI-Driven Testing & Development

 - Implement and scale AI-assisted test case generation from product requirements, user stories, and production data

 - Leverage LLM-based tools to generate, refactor, and maintain Playwright test suites

 - Build workflows for automated test creation, prioritization, and gap detection using AI

 - Evaluate and integrate tools for self-healing tests, intelligent selectors, and failure triaging

 - Partner with engineering to incorporate AI into broader development workflows (code generation, debugging, QA automation)

 - Establish guardrails and validation strategies to ensure correctness and determinism in AI-generated tests

 

Required Qualifications

 - 5+ years in QA automation, SDET, or software engineering roles

 - Deep expertise with Playwright (2+ years required) in production environments

 - Strong proficiency in TypeScript/JavaScript

 - Experience designing scalable test frameworks from scratch

 - Solid understanding of CI/CD systems and modern DevOps practices

 - Experience testing API-driven architecture and distributed systems

source_jobs.title: Lead Relationship Manager
source_jobs.description:
About PactFi

Private asset markets (PE, Private Credit, VC, Real Estate) have 10x to 9.8T in AUM over the past decade and are projected to grow to $17T in the next five years. However, digital infrastructure has not kept pace, with most of the market operating predominantly in error-prone, internal-only software solutions.



PactFi provides secure, end-to-end, operational infrastructure for managing complex private credit transactions. Our web-based application brings together all parties involved in such a transaction to more efficiently allocate capital, complete KYC, share documents, manage funds flow, and more. The platform is secured to a bank-grade standard, and we have received our SOC 2 Type 2 attestation.



PactFi was developed in close partnership with two of the industry's largest players, both of whom represent the top 3 players in the private credit space by both size (AUM) and deal activity.



About the Role
We’re looking for a strategic Lead Relationship Manager to own and deepen relationships with our institutional clients in the private credit space. As a core member of our Business Development team, you'll take the handoff from our Account Executives — owning the client relationship and making sure every account reaches its full potential.

This is a land-and-expand role at its core. Once we win a client, you’re responsible for making sure they stay, grow, and get more value out of PactFi over time. You’ll be accountable for retention, attrition, and expansion across your book — and you’ll have the relationships, the data, and the product knowledge to deliver on all three.

You don’t need to have spent your career in private credit — but you’re the type of person who picks up a new market quickly, asks the right questions, and earns credibility with sophisticated investors through preparation and commercial instinct.





WHAT YOU’LL DO:


Client Relationships & Engagement
• Serve as the primary relationship owner for a portfolio of our private credit clients, building deep relationships with senior stakeholders and decision-makers.
• Lead Executive Business Reviews and joint business planning sessions, facilitating strategic conversations that connect our product capabilities to each client’s evolving investment objectives.
• Navigate high-pressure conversations with credibility and commercial rigor — whether managing escalations, negotiating renewals, or presenting.




Retention, Adoption & Growth
• Own the full post-sale client lifecycle — driving product adoption, deepening engagement across PactFi’s suite of products, and identifying expansion opportunities within your book of business.
• Monitor client performance and health metrics proactively, taking decisive action to address risks and surface opportunities before they become issues.
• Partner with clients to ensure they are fully activated and generating value from the platform as efficiently as possible.




Strategy & Operations
• Use data, structured analysis, and AI-enabled tools to identify growth opportunities, improve program management, and continuously optimize outcomes across your portfolio.
• Build and maintain account plans that reflect each client’s strategic priorities, key stakeholders, and near-term opportunities — keeping internal teams aligned on account health and trajectory.
• Develop a prioritization framework for your portfolio based on revenue potential, engagement depth, and expansion opportunity.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING:

Experience
• 5+ years of experience in account management, client success, investment banking, consulting, or a related client-facing role within financial services — alternatives, private credit, asset management, or fintech serving institutional clients.
• Proven track record managing senior-level relationships with institutional investors, asset managers, or other sophisticated financial counterparties.
• Experience operating in a post-sale capacity, owning client outcomes after handoff from a sales o

• Experience operating in a post-sale capacity, owning client outcomes after handoff from a sales or business

notes: PactFi is a fintech startup with a strong engineering core, developing a web-based application for private credit transactions.
